summary: Frontend-only mobile Hero regression testing after the mobile responsive fix (min-h-[100svh], pt-28 pb-14 lg:pt-24 lg:pb-0, mobile H1 text-[2.35rem]). Verified at 3 mobile viewports (360x640, 375x667, 390x844), mobile nav, mobile smoke of all sections, and desktop 1920x800 regression. All acceptance criteria met.
critical_code_review_comments: Hero.jsx has a duplicated min-h-[100svh] on both the <section> and the inner content <motion.div>. On short screens (<= 640/667px viewport) this pushes the trust-bar off the visible fold (section grows to ~861px). Trust-bar is still reachable by scrolling but is not visible without any scroll on 360x640 / 375x667. If the intent is for the trust bar to be visible in the initial viewport on short phones, consider removing the min-h from the inner content wrapper and letting it collapse to its natural height (or reducing paddings further). Current behavior is acceptable and not a bug.

Console warning present on every page load: 'Please ensure that the container has a non-static position, like relative/fixed/absolute to ensure scroll offset is calculated correctly.' — likely from framer-motion useScroll on the hero background parallax. Non-blocking, but worth silencing by adding position: relative to the scroll target.
Carry-over from iteration_1/2 (still valid): /admin is public with PII; EnquiryCreate lacks max_length; POST /api/enquiries lacks rate-limit.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile Hero bug fix verified. Measured box positions at 375x667 mobile: nav bottom=86, hero-budget-banner y=112 (gap=26px, safe), hero-eyebrow y=180, hero-heading y=233 (h=116), hero-cta-quote y=483, hero-cta-projects y=547, trust-bar y=638 (in-scroll, but section height 861 accommodates it via min-h growth). At 390x844 all elements including trust-bar fit in the initial viewport. Desktop 1920x800: section#home height=815, trust-bar y=723 (fits in 800px viewport). No horizontal overflow on any tested viewport. Mobile nav (data-testid=nav-mobile-toggle) opens full-width white drawer with Home/Services/Projects/About Us/Process/Reviews. Note: About Us testid remains 'nav-showcase' (from prior iteration). #pricing section exists on the page (top=11670, height=902 at 375px).
rca of the issue: N/A — bug fix already applied by E1 and verified working. Original RCA (from problem statement): pt-44 + 12vw H1 made hero content taller than viewport, then overflow-hidden + fixed h-[100svh] caused the £10k-£100k banner to overlap the fixed navbar. Fix: min-h-[100svh] (allow grow), pt-28 pb-14 on mobile, H1 text-[2.35rem] mobile.
